In order to compute efficiently $y = a_1 \cdot x_1 + a_2 \cdot x_2 + ... $,
a practical syntax would be: 
`y = np.linear_combination(a,[x1,x2,...])` with `a` 1d-array and any `[x1, x2, ...]` of the same length","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27370/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27370/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2342807981","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27370#issuecomment-2342807981","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27370","id":2342807981,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86LpGmt","user":{"login":"bp208322","id":99986706,"node_id":"U_kgDOBfWtEg","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/99986706?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322","html_url":"https://github.com/bp208322","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bp208322/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-11T06:53:45Z","updated_at":"2024-09-11T06:53:45Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hereafter is an example where the linear combination is made by hand.
Maybe there exist some numpy functions doing the job but I don't see how.
```python
import numpy as np 

# elementary (3,3)-arrays
x1 = np.array([0,1,1, 0,0,1, 0,0,0]).reshape(3,3)
x2 = np.eye(3)
x3 = np.array([0,0,0, 1,0,0, 1,1,0]).reshape(3,3)
x = np.array([x1,x2,x3])
print('x=\n',x)

# coefficients
a = np.array([1,2,3])
print('a=\n',a)

# linear combination
y = (a[0]*x[0]) + (a[1]*x[1]) + (a[2]*x[2])
print('y=\n',y)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2342807981/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
